title: Publications
text:

title: Team
text:
Principal Investigator
      
      Assistant Professor at the University of Alberta · Amii Fellow
      
        Zhou Yang is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computing Science at the University of Alberta and an Amii Fellow. He completed his Ph.D. at Singapore Management University in December 2024, after earning an MSc from University College London. He previously served as a senior research engineer at the Centre for Research on Intelligent Software Engineering.
      
      
        His research establishes scientific and practical foundations for trustworthy code LLMs, integrating robustness, security, privacy, ecosystem understanding, and efficiency to support safer AI adoption in software engineering. He has received multiple major awards, including the 2026 MSR Outstanding Doctoral Research Award, the 2025 ACM SIGSOFT Outstanding Doctoral Dissertation Award, and the 2024 IEEE Computer Society Best Paper Award.
      
      
        Email
        Google Scholar
        Homepage

title: Vacancy
text:
I am looking for self-motivated students to join our group. If you are passionate about software engineering and AI, please read on!


Graduate Students (PhD / Master by Research)
Applications for Fall 2026 admission are now closed. However, if you are a very strong candidate, we may make exceptions on a case-by-case basis — please contact me at zy25@ualberta.ca with your CV, transcript, and a brief description of your research interests.



Research Assistant Positions for UofA Students
If you are a current University of Alberta student (e.g., a Master’s student in the ECE Department, or an undergraduate) and are interested in joining our group as a research assistant, please send your CV and transcript to zy25@ualberta.ca.



Undergraduate Individual Studies
If you are a UofA undergraduate interested in taking an individual study course (CMPUT 399 or CMPUT 499), please contact me at zy25@ualberta.ca with your CV and transcript.







NoteScreening Process



All prospective research assistants and individual study students will go through a lightweight screening process, which typically involves:

Paper reading — reading a few selected papers relevant to your intended research area
Presentation — giving a short talk to demonstrate your understanding of the papers
Implementation — reproducing or implementing some baseline methods

During this process, we will evaluate each student’s academic merits, prior research experience, and how well their background aligns with the group’s research directions. This helps us assess your research potential and ensures a good mutual fit.





Contact
For all inquiries, please reach out to Prof. Zhou Yang.

title: U-A-Goose
text:
We are the U-a-Goose (GrOup Of Software Engineering) group at University of Alberta.



  




U-A-Goose studies trustworthy software engineering for the AI era, with a particular focus on large language models for code, AI security and privacy, and efficient and sustainable AI systems.
Our projects sit at the intersection of software engineering and machine learning: robust coding assistants, evaluation and testing of AI-enabled systems, automated protection of critical software operations, specification-driven development, and emerging systems topics such as game compatibility layers.

  Trustworthy Code LLMs
  AI Security & Privacy
  Software Engineering
  Efficient AI Systems





Our group is based in the Department of Computing Science at the University of Alberta. Prof. Zhou Yang is an Amii Fellow, and the lab is connected to the Amii research ecosystem through collaborative projects and Amii-supported initiatives, including the recently approved Amii RAP funding.









[2026.04] Our paper, MultiCodeAttack: Iterative Jailbreak Attacking on LLMs with Multi-Code Prompt Injection, is accepted by ACL 2026!
[2026.04] My NSERC Discovery Grant has been approved! Grateful for the support to advance our research.
[2026.03] Dr. Zhou Yang’s Amii RAP funding (2026 to 2027) 💰 is approved, collaborating with Matt Taylor.
[2026.03] One paper on efficient code LLM inference with quantization and compilation-time optimization is accepted by FSE 2026.
[2026.03] Hanzheng Dai’s doctoral symposium paper on “Automated Diagnosis and Testing of Game Compatibility Layers” is accepted by FSE 2026 Doctoral Symposium.
[2026.03] Dr. Zhou Yang wins the MSR Outstanding Doctoral Research Award🏆! There is only one awardee this year.
[2026.01] One paper (on automatically protecting critical software operations in TEE) is accepted by IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering!
[2025.12] Dr. Zhou Yang wins the ACM SIGSOFT Outstanding Doctoral Dissertation Award🏆! There is only one award this year.
[2025.11] Our paper on understanding user perceptions of AI coding assistants wins the Distinguished Paper Award🏆 at ASE 2025!
[2025.09] Our paper wins the prestigious 2024 IEEE Computer Society Best Paper Award🏆! (1 out of 183)
[2025.09] Dr. Zhou Yang officially joins the University of Alberta as an Assistant Professor and becomes an Amii Fellow.
